notes: v1.14.130 - STAGE 4 / BUILD-2: inject captured Ghost-Echo quirks into the AI context. Site profile was already injected into every AI surface via activeContext_getContextBlock, but the tech's own logged tribal fixes (phantom_ghost_echo_v1, ge_saveQuirk) were NEVER in any AI prompt - only whispered under phase dots (ge_findQuirks). Now activeContext_getContextBlock() appends a 'FIELD QUIRKS' block via new activeContext_getQuirksBlock(activeHwType): pulls outgoing (site-scoped, tolerant ge_normSite) + incoming (cross-site imports, same rule as ge_findQuirks) quirks, dedupes, sorts active-rack-hardware-first -> echoes -> recency, caps at 8 (+N more) for token budget, formats '- [hardware · phase] text — author'. Because activeContext_getContextBlock feeds ALL AI surfaces (vaAsk, parsers, EDP validator, optic-ID, troubleshoot), the assistant now uses corrections this same tool captured. Reuse-don't-rebuild: wired the existing quirk store into the existing context-assembly fn. JS-only, no UI/markup, no new assets. Browser-verified: no quirks -> no block; after ge_saveQuirk('GB300','mechanical','...14 Nm...') the context block contains FIELD QUIRKS + the quirk text; zero console errors. node --check x3 + sw.js, brace gate, three-stamp lockstep. (BUILD-1 assistant entry already live as the Ask card; BUILD-3 serial-resurface deferred pending John.) DEVICE-VERIFY: log a quirk on a rack phase (Ghost Echo), then ask the Voice Assistant about that hardware/phase - the answer should reflect the logged quirk.
